summary: HouseBuildr is a Beverly Hills-based, licensed (CSLB# 1025901) solar installation and home-improvement contractor serving California homeowners and commercial property owners, with offerings spanning residential/commercial solar, panel maintenance, HVAC, roofing, and windows. It sells high-ticket, custom-quoted projects (residential solar typically $15k–$30k+) through a high-touch sales motion: free site assessment, custom proposal, financing, then installation. Its core promise is dramatically lower electricity bills and energy independence for upper-middle to affluent LA-area homeowners.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Turn a new quote/inquiry lead into a booked site assessment, and set expectations across the multi-week permitting-to-go-live journey for signed customers.
campaigns.trigger: Quote request / free-assessment form submitted on housebuildr.com.
campaigns.segment: New leads who requested a quote or downloaded a guide; branch by monthly-bill amount and homeowner vs. commercial.
campaigns.rationale: HouseBuildr has no sequence bridging inquiry to site assessment, and the aha moment (first low utility bill) lands 30–90 days post-install — the longest, riskiest gap in the funnel. A fast, warm welcome captures intent at its peak and books the one action that starts the deal.

emails.purpose: Instant warm welcome, set expectations for the solar journey, and drive the single most valuable first action: booking the free site assessment.
emails.subject: Welcome to HouseBuildr — your free solar assessment is the first step
emails.preview: Here's exactly what happens next (and what your roof could be saving you).
keyPoints: Thank them for reaching out and reaffirm the outcome they want: lower (or near-zero) LA electricity bills and energy independence
Demystify the process up front — site assessment, custom proposal, financing options, permits, then installation — so the multi-week timeline feels guided, not overwhelming
One clear CTA to book the free, no-obligation site assessment; reinforce trust with CSLB# 1025901, 10+ years, and 1000+ installations
Invite a real reply for questions; tease that the proposal will show their specific payback timeline

campaigns.name: Quote-to-Contract Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert free-quote and site-assessment recipients into signed contracts by removing the cost/ROI objection that stalls high-ticket solar decisions.
campaigns.trigger: Site assessment completed / custom proposal delivered but no signed contract.
campaigns.segment: Active opportunities with a proposal out; branch by engaged (opened proposal, asked questions) vs. stalled (no response).
campaigns.rationale: This is the core revenue play — HouseBuildr currently relies entirely on sales reps to close, with no email support. The #1 objection is 'that's a lot of money — how long until I break even?', so the hero email must answer payback directly with their own numbers.

emails.purpose: Directly handle the upfront-cost and ROI-timeline objection by quantifying payback and financing, moving the lead from interested to ready-to-sign.
emails.subject: How long until your solar pays for itself? Here's your real number
emails.preview: Most LA homeowners break even faster than they expect — let's run yours.
keyPoints: Lead with the math: against some of the highest utility rates in the US, monthly savings often cover the system and turn the roof into a return
Reframe upfront cost with $0-down financing options so the decision is monthly-savings vs. monthly-bill, not a $15k–$30k wall
Defuse installer distrust — licensed CSLB# 1025901, 1000+ installations, workmanship and post-install support spelled out
Low-friction next step: a quick call to walk through their specific proposal and lock in the timeline

campaigns.name: Site Assessment Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Maximize attended site assessments and installation appointments and recover the ones that slip.
campaigns.trigger: Site assessment or installation appointment confirmed; upcoming appointment; no-show event.
campaigns.segment: Anyone with a scheduled site assessment, install, or service visit.
campaigns.rationale: Site assessments are HouseBuildr's revenue event and are likely managed manually with no automated reminders — every no-show is a stalled high-ticket deal. Reminders cut no-shows ~35% on average, recovering pipeline immediately at near-zero cost.

emails.purpose: Day-before reminder that confirms the appointment, preps the homeowner, and offers a one-tap reschedule so a scheduling conflict becomes a rebook instead of a no-show.
emails.subject: See you tomorrow — your HouseBuildr solar assessment
emails.preview: A 2-minute prep so we can give you the most accurate quote.
keyPoints: Restate the appointment time, address, and the assessor's name to make it feel personal and real
Quick prep list — recent electricity bill on hand, roof/attic access, and any questions about financing or incentives
Reassure: it's free, no-obligation, and takes about an hour; set expectation for what they'll learn
Frictionless reschedule link to capture conflicts instead of losing the slot to a silent no-show

campaigns.name: Solar Savings Guide Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move guide downloaders who aren't yet ready for a quote toward sales-readiness over a weeks-long consideration cycle.
campaigns.trigger: Download of the energy/solar savings guide (refreshed for 2026 incentive schedules).
campaigns.segment: Leads who downloaded the guide but haven't booked an assessment; branch by homeowner vs. commercial interest.
campaigns.rationale: HouseBuildr's incentives guide isn't followed by any email sequence, and solar is a considered, weeks-to-months purchase where trust is earned through education. A stage-matched nurture warms cold downloaders and feeds qualified leads into the conversion campaign.

emails.purpose: Instantly deliver the guide and frame the real problem behind it — rising CA utility costs and the confusion that keeps homeowners from acting.
emails.subject: Your solar savings guide — and the real reason your LA bill keeps climbing
emails.preview: What CA utility rate hikes mean for your home (and how to get ahead of them).
keyPoints: Deliver the download immediately and confirm they're set
Frame the problem: California's utility rates are among the highest in the US and keep rising — waiting costs money every billing cycle
Position HouseBuildr as the trusted, licensed guide through the complexity (permits, financing, equipment, current 2026 incentives) rather than a hard sell
Soft next step — when they're ready, a free site assessment turns the guide's general numbers into their specific savings

campaigns.name: Cold Quote Win-Back
campaigns.objective: Reactivate warm leads who received a quote but never signed, and dormant past contacts, before they go to a competitor or go silent for good.
campaigns.trigger: 60–90 days of no engagement after a quote/assessment with no signed contract.
campaigns.segment: Aged quote recipients and dormant leads; separate branch for past customers who never expanded.
campaigns.rationale: HouseBuildr has no re-engagement of warm leads who got a quote but didn't convert — a large, high-intent pool already past the hardest step. Win-back recovers 5–15% of inactive contacts and protects sender reputation by sunsetting the rest.

emails.purpose: Re-open the conversation with lapsed quote leads by reminding them of the savings still on the table and what's changed since they last looked.
emails.subject: Still paying full price for power? Your solar quote is still good
emails.preview: Rates went up again — here's what your roof could be doing instead.
keyPoints: Acknowledge the gap warmly and lead with the cost of inaction: every month without solar is another full CA utility bill
Pattern interrupt — what's new since they last looked (updated 2026 incentives, financing options, battery storage adjacency)
Make returning easy: refresh their existing quote with no need to start over, no obligation
Reassurance via track record (licensed, 1000+ installs) to rebuild trust with a lead who cooled off
